name: AIUC-1 starter policy
note: A STARTING POINT, not an official certified mapping. The Runtime Record itself is the evidence for AIUC-1 E015 (Log AI system activity) — E015.1 activity logging, E015.2 agent-execution logging (tool calls + authorization + delegation via parent_id), E015.4 log integrity (the hash chain). These rules add deterministic policy corroboration over Halo Runtime Record v0.1 fields, tagged with the closest AIUC-1 control area. They are evidence that a control operated, not the preventive control itself. Scope of the corroboration: a rule only sees what the record already carries — the PII/secret rules corroborate over the deterministic scanner's named categories (see LIMITS.md), not a comprehensive PII detector, and threat/authorization fields are ingested or self-asserted by the agent (sealed into the tamper-evident chain, but not independently attested). A PASS therefore means 'no flagged item in scope for this rule', not 'independently verified absent'. Rename and tune them to your agent's tools and your buyer's contract. Run: halo policy <chain.jsonl> aiuc-1-starter.json  (or: halo report <chain.jsonl> --policy aiuc-1-starter.json).

rules.description: No credential or sensitive identifier may ever land in a record.

Records of network actions must not carry SSNs off the boundary. Corroborates over data.pii_types, which reflects the scanner's named categories (email/ssn/credit_card/phone/iban) — a PII shape the scanner does not detect will not appear here.
Every action that touches personal data declares why (purpose limitation).

No prompt-injection threat (ingested from an upstream guardrail) may appear unmitigated in the record. A PASS means no such flag is present in scope — it is not proof a detector ran; absence of a threat field is not evidence of a clean input.

rules.source: AIUC-1 C007 (flag high-risk outputs for human review)
rules.description: Any action the recorder marked CRITICAL is treated as out of policy until reviewed.

rules.source: AIUC-1 B006 / D003 (prevent unauthorized actions, restrict unsafe tool calls)
rules.description: Security-category write/network actions must carry an explicit authorization decision, never run unattributed.
TEMPLATE — expects evidence a human-escalation tool ran. Rename action.tool to your real escalation tool, or delete if not applicable.
